sources.title: EIA Electric Power Annual Table 2.2 workbook
EIA Electric Power Annual Table 2.8 workbook

prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: contact sheet for cropping into individual side-rail source-card photos
Primary request: Create an exact 2x2 grid of four equal photorealistic photos. Each panel must look like a separate original cinematic news-magazine photograph: cool, creative, specific, and visually energetic, not a boring desk still life, not an outlet close-up, not an illustration, not a collage, not CGI.
top-left: Source 001, EIA data. Create a rain-slick substation reflected in a puddle, with a distant data-center campus glowing beyond the fence. Connect it visually to EIA Electric Power Annual Table 2.2 workbook from U.S. Energy Information Administration. Use macro industrial detail with shallow depth of field, hard summer heat haze with realistic contrast, and black cable bundles, galvanized metal, and orange work lights. Do not render readable text.
top-right: Source 002, EIA data. Create a dense apartment block at night with staggered lit windows under high-voltage lines, shot from street level. Connect it visually to EIA Electric Power Annual Table 2.2 from U.S. Energy Information Administration. Use over-the-shoulder investigative newsroom perspective, dramatic side light through dust and vapor, and steel gray, wet asphalt, and utility amber. Do not render readable text.
bottom-left: Source 003, EIA data. Create a utility service alley behind restaurants where conduit, transformers, and steam vents crowd the frame. Connect it visually to EIA Electric Power Annual Table 2.8 workbook from U.S. Energy Information Administration. Use rain-reflection foreground with deep background scale, cold fluorescent interior light with one warm practical glow, and concrete, copper, and pale green equipment tones. Do not render readable text.
bottom-right: Source 004, EIA data. Create a drone-like overlook of a city edge where warehouses, homes, and transmission towers collide at sunset. Connect it visually to EIA Electric Power Annual Table 2.8 from U.S. Energy Information Administration. Use symmetrical control-room composition with human figures as silhouettes, hard summer heat haze with realistic contrast, and black cable bundles, galvanized metal, and orange work lights. Do not render readable text.
Style/medium: photorealistic editorial documentary photography with true camera texture, realistic depth of field, real industrial materials, believable human scale, and cinematic but plausible light.
Composition/framing: each panel should fill its own equal area edge to edge; keep the subject centered enough that it survives a horizontal 800 by 520 crop.
Lighting/mood: investigative, high-energy, atmospheric, and grounded in real utility/data-center infrastructure.
Constraints: brand-new original images generated from scratch; no reused article assets; no stock-photo look; no paper bills as the main subject; no generic wall outlets; no logos; no watermarks; no readable words; no readable numbers; no fake document layouts; no visible panel labels; no public figures. Every panel must be visibly different in setting, camera angle, lighting, and subject.
